commitMessage: Fix bots getting stuck in infinite loops; add starvation timer

topics.title: Add starvation timer so stuck bots eventually lose automatically
topics.trigger: After the loop guard was added to break bot oscillation cycles, the user raised a new concern: a bot that wanders productively but never reaches food could keep the game running indefinitely, forcing a human watcher to manually stop it. There needed to be a hard backstop that ends the game cleanly.
topics.response:
- Added per-snake move counters (`movesSinceFood1`, `movesSinceFood2`) in `src/main.ts` that increment each tick and reset to zero whenever that snake eats. A `starvationLimit()` helper computes the budget as `max(100, 2 Ă— boardArea)`, scaling with the playable area of the current board. When a counter reaches the limit, the snake is declared dead through the existing game-over paths â€” single-player calls `endGame()`, two-player feeds the starved snake as "dead" into `endGameTwoPlayer()`, supporting draws when both starve on the same tick. A latent bug was also fixed: `expandGrid()` doubled `gridSize` without refreshing `boardArea`, which would have made the starvation limit stale after a classic-mode grid expansion.
- Added a visual hunger tint in `draw()`: once a snake passes 60% of its move budget without eating, its colours blend toward an amber/red warning palette via a `lerpHex()` colour interpolator, ramping to full warning colour at the limit. Game-over text is updated to show "Starved!" (single-player) or named starvation outcomes ("Player 2 Wins! (P1 starved)", "Draw â€” both starved!") via a new `deathCause` field.
- Added a pulsing overlay banner (`#starvation-warning` in `index.html`, styled in `src/style.css`) that appears the instant the hunger tint begins and names which snake is at risk in two-player modes. The element is positioned over the canvas via a `.canvas-wrap` wrapper so it does not shift the game layout.
topics.decisions:
- **Area-based limit over length-based:** The budget scales with how large the playable board is rather than how long the snake is. A bigger board means food can legitimately be farther away, so the limit grows with the board. The user asked whether longer snakes should get more time (a natural assumption), and the decision was to keep the limit board-area-based with a reset-on-eat mechanic â€” a competent snake never approaches the limit, it only fires as a backstop when food is genuinely unreachable.
- **Reuse existing loss paths rather than a new death type:** Starvation is routed through the same `endGame` / `endGameTwoPlayer` calls already used for collision deaths, with a `deathCause` flag to adjust the displayed text. This kept the win/loss/draw resolution logic untouched and meant two-player starvation draws (both snakes starving on the same tick) were handled for free by the existing symmetry in `endGameTwoPlayer`.
- **Overlay banner over inline HUD text:** The warning is a pulsing pill overlaid on the canvas rather than a line added to the score HUD, so it grabs attention without shifting the game layout. A plain urgent message was chosen over a live countdown to avoid per-tick number flicker that could itself be distracting during play.

topics.title: Add loop guard to break bot oscillation cycles in tight board pockets
topics.trigger: In Iron Snake mode (and occasionally in classic mode), bots would get permanently stuck in an approach-and-retreat cycle: a bot would path toward food, correctly refuse to enter a tight pocket that would trap it, retreat, then deterministically re-select the exact same losing approach and repeat forever. The game would never end and the bot would never make progress.
topics.response:
- Created `src/bots/loop-guard.ts`, a new shared module that provides the memory and variation the stateless bot layer lacks. It maintains a rolling history of recent head positions per snake and detects loops via two signals: a tight oscillation (any cell revisited 3+ times in a 40-move window) and a confinement ratio (too few distinct cells relative to moves over the window, catching larger circuits that the revisit counter alone misses). When a loop is detected, `chooseEscapeDirection` picks the legal, non-trapping move whose destination cell has been visited least recently, with random tie-breaking, and commits to that escape direction for 8 ticks.
- Wired the loop guard into both bot harnesses in `src/main.ts` (`chooseBotDirection` for single-player and `chooseBotDirectionForPlayer` for two-player), with per-snake `LoopMemory` fields. `resetLoopMemory` is called at every progress point: food eaten, level advance, snake reposition, and new game/restart. The same wiring was applied to the title-screen background animation in `src/menu-demo.ts` using the same shared module.
topics.decisions:
- **Harness-level guard over per-bot changes:** All 11 bots are stateless pure functions that return only a direction â€” they have no mechanism to store history or vary their scoring across ticks. Adding memory to each bot would have required changing the shared bot interface and rewriting every bot. A centralized guard at the harness level intercepts the chosen direction only when a loop is actually detected, leaving all bot personalities completely intact during normal play.
- **Confinement ratio added after revisit-count alone proved insufficient:** Initial testing showed that a pure revisit counter (3 hits in a window) cannot catch a ~20-cell "approach, give up, wander back" circuit â€” on a period-20 loop, no cell recurs 3 times until 60 moves have passed. A secondary confinement signal (distinct cells / total moves below a threshold over a 24-move sample) was added to catch these larger circuits, confirmed by a simulation where the guard broke a bot out of a 20-cell ring to explore 125 cells.

recap:
Bots in Iron Snake mode could previously get locked into permanent approach-and-retreat cycles, approaching a food cell, refusing to enter a tight pocket, retreating, and then repeating the exact same sequence indefinitely. A new loop detection system now tracks each bot's recent movement history and, when it recognises the snake is circling rather than making progress, steers it toward the least-recently-visited safe cell for several ticks to break the cycle. All bot personalities are preserved during normal play â€” the override only activates when a genuine loop is detected. The same fix was applied to the animated snakes on the title screen.

A complementary starvation system was added to handle the remaining edge case: a bot that wanders productively but can never reach walled-off food. Each snake now has a move budget between meals that scales with the size of the playable board. If a snake exhausts its budget without eating, it dies and the game ends cleanly with a "Starved!" result screen. In two-player modes, the outcome correctly names which snake starved, and simultaneous starvation resolves as a draw. As a snake approaches its limit, its colour gradually shifts from green toward amber and red, and a pulsing warning banner appears over the game board so players know to race for the food.

plans.title: Fix bot infinite-loop / oscillation (esp. Iron Snake Mode)
